channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Public footpath from Dene Av to Marlborough park adjacent to Sidcup Golf Club needs cutting back and clearing to make it safe for walkers, 5th May
item.description:
The public footpath from Dene Avenue to Marlborough park that runs adjacent to Sidcup Golf Club needs cutting back and clearing to make it safe for walkers. It is a disgraceful mess as are all the public footpaths in the London Borough of Bexley. The public have a right to walk these paths without being stung by vastly overgrown nettles, cut by nasty blackberry thorns and dangerously low overhanging branches. If the London Borough of Bexley insist on ignoring the over-bloated problem of road traffic pollution, at least give those of us who do give a damn some clean footpaths to make our way back and forth to work on foot.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hurst Road, Albany Park, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown Tree Pushing Up Pavement = Trip Hazard, 5th May
item.description:
This tree should have been removed 10-15 years ago. Outside 131 Greenvale Road, Eltham, SE9 It is overgrown, damaging the tarmac pavement In 1987 trees this size ended up inside people's upstairs front rooms on Greenvale Road and elsewhere

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Greenvale Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned car, 5th May
item.description:
Has been abandoned and stationary for months. It has a tarpaulin covering it sometimes but falls off in wind. Parts have fallen off underneath.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brookdene Road, Plumstead

item.title: Fly tipping of household waste, 5th May
item.description:
Yet again the residents of 57 Edmeston Close think it is ok to dump their old mattresses and bedding etc onto the pavement.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Edmeston Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Untaxed van with smashed side window, 5th May
item.description:
Van has been parked here all morning and is obstructing the Tredwell close junction. It also has a motion alarm that is constantly going off.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southlands Road, Bickley, Greater London

item.title: Toilet flush broken, 5th May

item.title: Dog walkers with large packs of dogs, 5th May
item.description:
Lots of dog walkers use this woodland but more recently there have been huge packs of dogs - 15 dogs counted today. The dogs were not responding to recall but were off the lead. Dog walkers tend to be walking in groups together as well which means, even if they’re sticking to the dog walking limits, there’s a large pack together which isn’t fair on other dogs who are barraged by them. Please can we have checks on dog licences and dog walkers in the woodland.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beaconsfield Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin shoot room, 5th May
item.description:
Flat 18 3rd floor keeps fly tipping in 2nd floors bin room ive told caretaker and he told me to send you guys the pictures

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Blendon Terrace, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet
